questions.question: 1 (a) One student places the metre rule on the pivot at the 50.0 cm mark and then places block U with its centre at the 5.0 cm mark. Suggest why it might be difficult to place block U accurately at the 5.0 cm mark. Explain how the student could overcome this difficulty. You may draw a diagram. ...........................................................................

parts.text: 1 (a) One student places the metre rule on the pivot at the 50.0 cm mark and then places block U with its centre at the 5.0 cm mark. Suggest why it might be difficult to place block U accurately at the 5.0 cm mark. Explain how the student could overcome this difficulty. You may draw a diagram. ...........................................................................
parts.answer: (difficult to see centre of block) and valid method, e.g. • (measure width of block and) add ½ width to 5.0 cm to find position for edge of block • mean value of marks at both edges of mass • mark centre line of mass and align with mark on rule move block back and forth to find the point of balance / owtte axes labelled correct orientation, with quantity and unit appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid) plots all correct to less than ½ small square and precise plots well-judged line and thin line G present and triangle method seen on graph M U in range 61.0 to 81.0 (g) 2/3 sig figs and unit

a and b are proportional b / a constant within limits of experimental accuracy / owtte 0625/63 May/June 2019 (°C) s, °C, °C, °C all correct 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180 any one from: • place stop-clock and thermometers so seen easily • keep eyes at level to thermometer scales • use an audible 30 s alarm • read temperatures alternately every 15 s • use of data logger

greater temperature difference causes greater (rate of) heating comparison of temperature changes during same peri od in first half and in second half of experiment within range 64 to 72 (°C) inclusive justification with reference to hot and cold water trends (°C) / room temperature two from: • thinner walls on tube • metal tube • bung/lid on tube • insulate sides of beaker/lid on beaker • higher initial hot water temperature • lower initial cold water temperature • increase volume of hot water • decrease volume of cold water • stirring 0625/63 May/June 2019
correct voltmeter symbol in parallel with X

statement matching results and ‘currents the same within limits of experimental accuracy’ / owtte R values 5.0/ecf, 14(.3478), 24/(23.8095) ( Ω ) consistent 2 or consistent 3 sig figs R Y = 9.0 ( Ω ) and R Z = 10 ( Ω ) 3 resistors in parallel arrangement and circuit symbols correct voltmeter and ammeter in correct arrangement and circuit symbols correct R P = R S 0625/63 May/June 2019 MP1 Apparatus metre rule / measuring tape MP2 Method drop ball from measured height measure height of bounce repeat for different height of release MP3 Precaution any one from: • repeat (for each height of release) and average • measure to same part of ball each time • measure height of bounce at eye level • release without throwing/impeding • use of video (for height of bounce) MP4 Control variable any one from: • same (diameter/mass/material) ball • type of floor covering MP5 Table columns for release height and bounce height and units MP6 Analysis any one from: • suitable analysis of readings • draw a suitable graph of drop height against bounce height MP7 Additional point any one from: • additional control variable • at least 5 sets of data taken • repeat experiment for different diameter of ball/floor covering • automatic release to eliminate differences 0625/63 May/June 2019
